paper_title: Artificial Soiling Tests and PV Soiling Losses Assessment

methods.description: This method involves performing artificial soiling tests using pollen on glass substrates to understand and quantify soiling issues under controlled laboratory conditions.

steps.description: Soda lime float glass coupons (approx. 4 × 4 × 3 mm) were used as substrates. Their surface areas were accurately measured with a caliper gauge.
steps.searchable_summary: measure surface area of glass coupons using caliper gauge

steps.description: Sample weighting was performed with a microbalance Sartorius TE64 with a precision of 0.1 mg.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh samples using Sartorius TE64 microbalance

steps.description: Hemispheric transmittance spectra at near-normal incidence were obtained from 300 nm to 1800 nm by means of a Perkin Elmer Lambda ® 900 spectrophotometer equipped with a Spectralon ® -coated LabSphere ® 150 mm integrating sphere.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain hemispheric transmittance spectra using spectrophotometer

steps.description: The glass coupons were cleaned, measured, and weighted and their transmittance spectra were obtained.
steps.searchable_summary: clean, measure, weigh, and obtain transmittance spectra of glass coupons

steps.description: They were used as substrates to collect fresh cypress (Cupressus sp.) pollen samples from CIEMAT's gardens.
steps.searchable_summary: collect cypress pollen samples on glass substrates

steps.description: The soiled substrates were weighted and analyzed optically again.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh and analyze optically the soiled substrates

steps.description: The corresponding pollen mass densities (ρA) were calculated as ρA = ΔM/A, where ΔM is the deposited pollen mass and A is the area of the glass coupon.

methods.method_name: Experimental Assessment of PV Soiling Losses
methods.description: This method involves setting up and monitoring an off-grid PV system to assess soiling losses under Mediterranean climate conditions.

steps.description: An off-grid PV system was installed on a 10m-high rooftop at the Research Centre for Energy, Environment and Technology (CIEMAT), Madrid, Spain.
steps.searchable_summary: install off-grid PV system on rooftop

steps.description: The PV system consists of twelve commercial PV modules with their corresponding temperature sensors, a PVPM2540C I-V curve tracer connected to a multiplexer, a DustIQ soiling monitoring sensor, and additional apparatus to monitor meteorological variables.
steps.searchable_summary: set up PV system with sensors and monitoring equipment

steps.description: The design of this PV system will allow us to determine the local soiling ratio and the influence of the local meteorology, as well as the dependence of soiling on several technical parameters.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze soiling ratio and meteorological influence
